name: Las Tejoneras Loop from Almadén de la Plata
name_original: Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla – circular por la Dehesa de San Bartolomé desde Almadén de la Plata
description: Hike the moderate 9.4-mile Las Tejoneras Loop from Almadén de la Plata, featuring aromatic forests and scenic viewpoints in Sierra Norte de
region_intro: Hiking around Almadén De La Plata offers diverse trails within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. The landscape features dehesas, Mediterranean scrubland, and unique granite formations, alongside several rivers and streams. This region provides a network of paths through rolling hills and wooded areas, suitable for various hiking abilities. Best hiking trails around Almadén De La Plata The most popular hiking route is Cerro del Calvario and El Chorro Waterfall Loop, a 10.4 miles (16.8 km) trail that takes 4 hours 39 minutes to complete. This moderate route includes views of the Cascada El Chorro. Another top favourite among local hikers is Las Tejoneras Loop from Almadén de la Plata, a moderate 9.4 miles (15.1 km) path. This trail winds through the characteristic dehesa landscape of the natural park. Local hikers also love the El Chorro Waterfall – Almadén de la Plata loop from Cerro Montes, a 7.9 miles (12.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain including the Arroyo de la Calzadilla, often completed in about 3 hours 30 minutes. Hiking around Almadén De La Plata is defined by dehesas, Mediterranean scrubland, and river valleys. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes. The routes in Almadén De La Plata are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.2 stars from more than 60 reviews. More than 200 hikers have used komoot to explore Almadén De La Plata's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Almadén De La Plata?
A: Almadén De La Plata offers a network of over 10 hiking routes, catering to various skill levels within the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park. These trails range from easy walks to more challenging moderate routes.

Q: What natural attractions can I expect to see while hiking in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The region is rich in natural beauty. A prominent feature is the &lt;strong&gt;Cascada El Chorro&lt;/strong&gt;, a picturesque waterfall in a narrow gorge. You can also explore the unique granite formations of the &lt;strong&gt;El Berrocal Natural Reserve&lt;/strong&gt; and enjoy the serene banks of the Cala and Viar rivers, as well as several arroyos like the Arroyo de la Calzadilla.

Q: Are there any viewpoints with panoramic vistas in Almadén De La Plata?
A: Yes, the &lt;strong&gt;Mirador Cerro del Calvario&lt;/strong&gt; offers breathtaking panoramic views of the surrounding meadows, rolling hills, and orchards. It's a popular and accessible destination, with routes like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1010137992/cerro-del-calvario-and-el-chorro-waterfall-loop" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cerro del Calvario and El Chorro Waterfall Loop&lt;/a&gt; leading to its summit.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, where Almadén De La Plata is located, is generally pleasant for hiking during spring and autumn. These seasons offer milder temperatures, ideal for exploring the dehesas and Mediterranean scrubland. Summers can be very hot, while winters are generally mild but can have occasional rain.

Q: Are there any easy hiking routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, there are several easy routes. For example, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/17265688" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall loop from El Chorro&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 3.2 km trail that allows you to experience the natural beauty of the area, including the waterfall, without significant elevation changes.

Q: Can I hike with my dog in Almadén De La Plata?
A: Many trails in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park are dog-friendly, allowing you to enjoy the outdoors with your canine companion. However, it's always advisable to keep dogs on a leash, especially in areas with livestock or wildlife, and to check specific trail regulations if available.

Q: Are there circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, many of the trails around Almadén De La Plata are designed as circular routes, allowing you to start and end at the same point. Popular options include the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e983588760/las-tejoneras-loop-from-almaden-de-la-plata" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Las Tejoneras Loop from Almadén de la Plata&lt;/a&gt; and the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/14621132" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Chorro Waterfall – Almadén de la Plata loop from Cerro Montes&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The El Berrocal Natural Reserve, in particular, is a haven for wildlife. Hikers might spot significant populations of deer and wild boars. The area is also home to various bird species, including the eagle owl and the imperial eagle, making it a great spot for birdwatching.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy most about the trails in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The trails in Almadén De La Plata are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.2 stars from over 60 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the diverse terrain, the characteristic dehesa landscapes, and the refreshing natural landmarks like Cascada El Chorro.

Q: Are there any historical sites or ancient ruins to explore near the hiking routes?
A: Yes, the area blends natural beauty with historical intrigue. You can find the site of &lt;strong&gt;Los Covachos&lt;/strong&gt;, which features a Neolithic settlement and ancient Roman quarries. Additionally, &lt;strong&gt;La Traviesa&lt;/strong&gt; is an Iberian archaeological site with a Bronze Age Necropolis, offering a captivating mix of history and scenic views.

Q: What are the typical difficulty levels of hikes in Almadén De La Plata?
A: The hiking network in Almadén De La Plata offers a range of difficulties. You'll find easy, well-maintained paths suitable for all, as well as moderate routes that may include more significant elevation changes and cover longer distances, requiring a good level of fitness.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details vary by trailhead, Almadén De La Plata is generally accessible by car, and you can typically find parking in or near the town before heading out on your hike. It's advisable to check local signage for any restrictions.
tips:
The Mirador Cerro del Calvario is a beautiful observation point located in the Sierra Norte de Sevilla Natural Park, near Almadén de la Plata. This viewpoint offers spectacular views of the region and is a popular hiking spot.
The route to the viewpoint is moderate and accessible for most fitness levels. Also near the viewpoint is El Chorro Waterfall, another natural attraction worth visiting.
This viewpoint is part of a network of trails and viewpoints that cross areas of great ecological and cultural value.

faqs.question: Where can I park to access the Las Tejoneras Loop?
faqs.answer: The trail starts and ends in Almadén de la Plata. You can typically find parking within the town itself, as it serves as the main access point for this loop.

faqs.question: Is the Las Tejoneras Loop dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While the sources don't explicitly state dog-friendliness, trails in natural parks often allow dogs on a leash. It's always best to check local regulations for Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla before heading out with your pet.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to hike this loop?
faqs.answer: The trail is particularly spectacular after rainfall, as the water features become more prominent and beautiful. This suggests that late autumn, winter, and early spring, when rainfall is more common, could be ideal times to experience the route at its most vibrant. Always check weather conditions before you go.

faqs.question: Are there any permits or entrance fees required for hiking in Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla?
faqs.answer: Generally, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required for hiking on public trails within the Parque Natural Sierra Norte de Sevilla, including the Las Tejoneras Loop. However, always respect park regulations and leave no trace.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: You'll hike through a varied and aromatic landscape featuring cork oaks, holm oaks, eucalyptus, and pine trees. The presence of rockrose (jara) and rosemary adds to the fragrant atmosphere. After rain, charming watery spots enhance the experience.

faqs.question: Is the Las Tejoneras Loop suitable for families?
faqs.answer: Yes, this loop is considered suitable for families. Its moderate difficulty and engaging natural environment make it a good option for a family outing, provided everyone has appropriate footwear and clothing.

faqs.question: Are there any notable viewpoints or landmarks along the route?
faqs.answer: Yes, the route offers several scenic spots. You can enjoy views from the Cerro del Calvario Viewpoint and take in the expansive View of the Dehesa Valley within the Parque Natural Sierra Morena de Sevilla.

faqs.question: Does the Las Tejoneras Loop connect with other trails?
faqs.answer: Yes, sections of this route overlap with other official trails. You'll find it intersects with parts of the Sendero de Sierra Morena, Transandalus, Mtb Vía de la Plata, Vía de la Plata hiking - 01 Sevilla to Merida, and Camino a Almadén.

faqs.question: What should I wear or bring for this hike?
faqs.answer: Given its moderate difficulty and varied terrain, it's recommended to wear appropriate clothing and sturdy hiking or mountain footwear. Bringing water, snacks, and sun protection is also advisable.

faqs.question: Is this a good trail for beginner hikers?
faqs.answer: Rated as having a 'medium' difficulty level, this trail is generally accessible. While not overly strenuous, beginners should be prepared for a hike of around 13 kilometers and ensure they have suitable gear.

faqs.question: Are there places to eat or drink in Almadén de la Plata after the hike?
faqs.answer: Almadén de la Plata is a charming town that serves as the starting and end point for the loop. You can expect to find local cafes, bars, and restaurants where you can refuel and relax after your hike.

waypoints.name: View of the Dehesa Valley, Parque Natural Sierra Morena de Sevilla
